content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Jul 22, 2026, 3:12 PM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

new page for introducing mcp capabilities and SEO purposes.
plan for it
I'll explore the existing homepage, MCP docs, and how similar SEO pages are built so the plan fits the site.

Before locking the plan, two choices change the page shape a lot:

1. **Primary audience** — who is this page for?
   - **A.** Job seekers using Claude/Cursor (product story: tailor CVs via MCP, connect in a few steps)
   - **B.** Developers / MCP directory SEO (tools list, transports, OAuth, connector URL)
   - **C.** Both: seeker-first narrative + a short “connect & capabilities” technical section

2. **URL** — preferred slug?
   - `/mcp` (short, brandable)
   - `/mcp-assistant` or `/claude-mcp` (more keyword-explicit)

My defaults if you don’t care: **C** + **`/mcp`**. Reply with A/B/C and a slug (or “defaults”) and I’ll write the concrete plan.

<timestamp>Wednesday, Jul 22, 2026, 3:34 PM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

ok
Using defaults: seeker-first page with a short connect/capabilities section at `/mcp`. Drafting the plan.

input.name: MCP SEO page
input.overview: Add a public marketing page at `/mcp` that introduces Kuchup’s Claude/Cursor MCP assistant for SEO and product discovery, following the existing homepage static-export + Flask sitemap pattern.
input.plan:
# MCP capabilities page (`/mcp`)

## Defaults locked

- **Audience:** seeker-first narrative + short technical “connect & capabilities” section
- **URL:** [`https://kuchup.com/mcp`](https://kuchup.com/mcp)
- **Design:** Warm Horizon from [`design.md`](design.md) — same shell as [`homepage/app/how-it-works/page.tsx`](homepage/app/how-it-works/page.tsx) / pricing (Header, Footer, tokens). Marketing page may use light enrichment; no invented metrics.

## Content structure

Single long-document marketing page (not a docs dump). Sections:

1. **Hero** — Brand-forward: Kuchup + “MCP for job applications”. One headline, one supporting line, CTAs: Sign in / Connect in workspace (`/apply` after login) and secondary to `/how-it-works`.
2. **What it does** — Job seekers: pin roles → agent loads JD from catalog → gated CV reframe → tailored LaTeX/PDF in workspace → you submit. Explicit: does **not** auto-apply.
3. **How to connect** — Public connector URL `https://mcp.kuchup.com/mcp` (Claude custom connectors + Cursor); point to panel **Connect MCP** on `/apply` for OAuth / tokens. Local stdio mentioned briefly for power users (no env secrets in copy).
4. **Capabilities** — Grouped capability chips/sections (not a full tool table):
   - Application queue & job context (JD from catalog)
   - Master + project resumes, gated reframe pipeline
   - Tailored CV / cover letter → panel PDF
   - Catalog helpers: add company / position, mark applied
5. **FAQ** — SEO + honesty: What is MCP? Do I need Claude/Cursor? Is my data private? Will it submit applications? Source: tone of [`docs/reference/mcp-application.md`](docs/reference/mcp-application.md) + existing HomeFAQ voice.
6. **Closing CTA** — Sign in → `/panel` or `/apply`.

## SEO wiring

- Next `metadata` + Open Graph (`url: https://kuchup.com/mcp`), title/description targeting phrases like “Claude MCP job applications”, “Cursor MCP resume”, “visa-sponsored job MCP”.
- Page-level **FAQPage** JSON-LD (in addition to root Organization/WebSite in layout).
- Register path in Flask so sitemap + catch-all serve it:
  - Add `"/mcp"` to `FIXED_MARKETING_PATHS` in [`relocation_jobs/web/server.py`](relocation_jobs/web/server.py) (feeds `robots.txt` → `sitemap.xml` via `public_marketing_paths()`).
- Update [`docs/operations/seo-indexing.md`](docs/operations/seo-indexing.md) to list `/mcp` under manual indexing requests.
- Footer Product links: add MCP in [`homepage/components/Footer.tsx`](homepage/components/Footer.tsx). Optional: one line/link from homepage FAQ or AccessSection — keep light (footer + sitemap is enough for v1).

## Implementation files

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| Create | [`homepage/app/mcp/page.tsx`](homepage/app/mcp/page.tsx) — page + metadata + FAQ JSON-LD |
| Optional split | `homepage/components/Mcp*.tsx` only if page grows past ~how-it-works size |
| Edit | [`relocation_jobs/web/server.py`](relocation_jobs/web/server.py) — `FIXED_MARKETING_PATHS` |
| Edit | [`homepage/components/Footer.tsx`](homepage/components/Footer.tsx) |
| Edit | [`docs/operations/seo-indexing.md`](docs/operations/seo-indexing.md) |
| Build | `cd homepage && npm run build:flask` → static HTML under `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/mcp.html` |

## Tests / verify

- Extend sitemap assertion in [`tests/web/test_public_api.py`](tests/web/test_public_api.py) so `/mcp` appears when marketing HTML exists (same pattern as country paths), or a focused route test that `FIXED_MARKETING_PATHS` includes `/mcp`.
- Local smoke: panel serves `/mcp` after homepage build; `GET /sitemap.xml` includes `https://kuchup.com/mcp`.

## Out of scope

- Live MCP tool catalog API for the page
- Changing OAuth / `mcp.kuchup.com` transport
- Deep skill docs (keep linking to GitHub `mcp-application.md` if needed)
- Header primary nav change (homepage anchors stay; footer + sitemap for discoverability)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  NextPage["homepage/app/mcp/page.tsx"] -->|build:flask| StaticHtml["static/homepage/mcp.html"]
  Flask["FIXED_MARKETING_PATHS + /mcp"] --> StaticHtml
  Flask --> Sitemap["/sitemap.xml"]
  Footer --> NextPage
```

todos.content: Create homepage/app/mcp/page.tsx with seeker-first copy, connect section, FAQ + JSON-LD metadata
Add /mcp to FIXED_MARKETING_PATHS; cover in public API / sitemap test
Footer link + seo-indexing.md; run homepage build:flask

Those roles become the queue your agent can load — with company, title, and catalog job description.", }, { title: "Reframe with checkpoints", body: "In Claude or Cursor, the agent runs a gated CV reframe: one phase at a time, with your approval before the next. Kuchup’s MCP server exposes your board, resumes, and application artifacts so the agent can prepare materials — not browse the open web for you.", }, { question: "Do I need Claude or Cursor?", answer: "Yes for the interactive reframe flow. You review the PDF and submit on the employer’s site yourself.", }, ] as const; export const metadata: Metadata = { title: "MCP for Claude and Cursor", description: "Connect Kuchup’s MCP server to Claude or Cursor to tailor CVs for visa-sponsored software roles. Load job context from the catalog, run a gated reframe pipeline, and keep PDFs in your workspace — without auto-apply.", openGraph: { title: "MCP for Claude and Cursor | Relocation Jobs", description: "Claude MCP and Cursor MCP for job applications: queue roles, reframe your CV with approval checkpoints, and render PDFs in Kuchup.", url: "https://kuchup.com/mcp", siteName: "Relocation Jobs", type: "website", }, }; export default function McpPage() { const faqJsonLd = { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", mainEntity: FAQ_ITEMS.map((item) => ({ "@type": "Question", name: item.question, acceptedAnswer: { "@type": "Answer", text: item.answer, }, })), }; return (

def test_public_seo_endpoints_are_available(v2_client):
    robots = v2_client.get("/robots.txt")
    assert robots.status_code == 200
    assert "Sitemap:" in robots.get_data(as_text=True)

    sitemap = v2_client.get("/sitemap.xml")
    assert sitemap.status_code == 200
    assert "<loc>https://kuchup.com/</loc>" in sitemap.get_data(as_text=True)
input.new_string:
def test_public_seo_endpoints_are_available(v2_client):
    robots = v2_client.get("/robots.txt")
    assert robots.status_code == 200
    assert "Sitemap:" in robots.get_data(as_text=True)

    sitemap = v2_client.get("/sitemap.xml")
    assert sitemap.status_code == 200
    body = sitemap.get_data(as_text=True)
    assert "<loc>https://kuchup.com/</loc>" in body
    assert "<loc>https://kuchup.com/mcp</loc>" in body


def test_mcp_marketing_path_serves_when_html_exists(v2_client, monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    from relocation_jobs.web import server as web_server

    html_dir = tmp_path / "homepage"
    html_dir.mkdir()
    (html_dir / "mcp.html").write_text("<html>mcp page</html>", encoding="utf-8")
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_server, "HOMEPAGE_STATIC", html_dir)

    resp = v2_client.get("/mcp")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "mcp page" in resp.get_data(as_text=True)

print('positions', [(x['title'], x['company_name'], x.get('location')) for x in p.get('positions',[])[:5]])

"
done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"

# Snapshot country labels from Redis/Postgres into homepage/data/countries.json
# before the static Next export. New marketing pages appear after this rebuild.
python3 "$ROOT/scripts/export_homepage_countries.py"
python3 "$ROOT/scripts/export_homepage_country_snapshots.py"

cd "$ROOT/homepage"
if [[ ! -d node_modules ]]; then
  npm install --silent
fi
npm run build:flask --silent

input.new_string:
def test_country_marketing_path_serves_exported_html(v2_client, monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    from relocation_jobs.web import server as web_server

    html_dir = tmp_path / "homepage"
    html_dir.mkdir()
    (html_dir / "relocation-jobs-armenia.html").write_text("<html>armenia</html>", encoding="utf-8")
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_server, "HOMEPAGE_STATIC", html_dir)

    missing_html = v2_client.get("/relocation-jobs-germany")
    assert missing_html.status_code == 404

    ok = v2_client.get("/relocation-jobs-armenia")
    assert ok.status_code == 200
    assert "armenia" in ok.get_data(as_text=True)

    sitemap = v2_client.get("/sitemap.xml")
    body = sitemap.get_data(as_text=True)
    assert "<loc>https://kuchup.com/relocation-jobs-armenia</loc>" in body
    assert "relocation-jobs-germany" not in body
input.old_string:
def test_country_marketing_path_requires_label_and_html(v2_client, monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    from relocation_jobs.web import server as web_server

    html_dir = tmp_path / "homepage"
    html_dir.mkdir()
    (html_dir / "relocation-jobs-armenia.html").write_text("<html>armenia</html>", encoding="utf-8")
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_server, "HOMEPAGE_STATIC", html_dir)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        web_server,
        "all_country_labels",
        lambda: {"armenia": "Armenia", "germany": "Germany"},
    )

    missing_html = v2_client.get("/relocation-jobs-germany")
    assert missing_html.status_code == 404

    ok = v2_client.get("/relocation-jobs-armenia")
    assert ok.status_code == 200
    assert "armenia" in ok.get_data(as_text=True)

    sitemap = v2_client.get("/sitemap.xml")
    body = sitemap.get_data(as_text=True)
    assert "<loc>https://kuchup.com/relocation-jobs-armenia</loc>" in body
    assert "relocation-jobs-germany" not in body

content.text:
Country pages now pull **real catalog data** for that country — not just swapped labels.

Each page shows:
- Company / role / sponsorship-positive **counts**
- **Cities** from that country’s listings
- Sample **companies** and sponsorship-positive **roles**
- Live refresh from `/api/public/preview?country=…`

So Germany surfaces numbers like 107 companies + ABOUT YOU / Berlin; Netherlands shows 49 + Adyen / Amsterdam; UK (empty catalog locally) says the board is still thin.

Hard-refresh e.g. http://127.0.0.1:5051/relocation-jobs-germany vs http://127.0.0.1:5051/relocation-jobs-netherlands.

input.description: Rebuild and verify distinct visa keywords per country

content.text:
Country pages are now **visa/sponsor explainers**, not company dumps.

Each market has its own story:
- **Germany** — EU Blue Card, employer declaration, BA path
- **Netherlands** — IND recognised sponsors + Highly Skilled Migrant
- **UK** — licensed sponsors + Certificate of Sponsorship
- **Ireland** — Critical Skills Employment Permit / DETE
- **Portugal** — Tech Visa certification + D3 / AIMA

Plus hubs, thresholds (with “verify official sources” disclaimers), country-specific flow steps, FAQ JSON-LD, and only a light catalog snapshot (counts + a few example names).

Hard-refresh e.g. http://127.0.0.1:5051/relocation-jobs-germany vs `/relocation-jobs-netherlands` vs `/relocation-jobs-uk`.
